Examples.Title: Cancel
Examples.Description: Cancel a pending image processing job. If the job has already completed, or if it is not in a cancellable state, this request will fail with a conflict error. Use this to prevent unnecessary costs for jobs started in error.
Examples.Code:
### Cancel
# @name cancelTask
DELETE {{host}}/image/v1/cancel/{{process_id}}
X-API-Key: {{api_key}}
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 204
# Description: The image processing job was successfully cancelled. No content returned.
# 400
# Description: The request contains malformed data in the body, path, or query parameters.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 401
# Description: Authentication is required and has failed or has not yet been provided.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 403
# Description: The API key doesn't have permissions to perform the request.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 404
# Description: The requested resource does not exist.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 409
# Description: The request conflicts with the current state.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 429
# Description: Too many requests hit the API too quickly. A backoff (e.g. exponential) is recommended for your requests.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 500
# Description: Unexpected error on our end.
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Denoise
Examples.Description:
Returns `process_id` and `eta` to use as an efficient way to track the progress of the image denoising job:
  - Use the `Status` endpoints to check the status of this job using the returned `process_id`.
  - Use the `Download` endpoints to download the input or processed image (once it is ready).
  - Use the `Cancel` endpoint to cancel the job while it is in progress.

The same `process_id` and `eta` are also returned in the response headers as `X-Process-ID` and `X-ETA` respectively.

> The `eta` represents when the job is expected to finish in Unix time. We recommend using this to know when you can expect your (bulk) jobs to finish processing.
Examples.Code:
### Denoise
# @name postDenoise
POST {{host}}/image/v1/denoise/async
X-API-Key: {{api_key}}
Content-Type: multipart/form-data
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Image processing request has been successfully created.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: The request contains malformed data in the body, path, or query parameters.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 401
# Description: Authentication is required and has failed or has not yet been provided.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 402
# Description: Payment is required to access the resource.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 403
# Description: The API key doesn't have permissions to perform the request.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 412
# Description: The preconditions to process the request were not met.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 413
# Description: The request is larger than the server is willing or able to process.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 415
# Description: The request entity has a media type which the server or resource does not support.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 422
# Description: The request was well-formed but was unable to be followed due to semantic errors.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 425
# Description: The request was sent too early to be processed. Please wait before retrying.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 429
# Description: Too many requests hit the API too quickly. A backoff (e.g. exponential) is recommended for your requests.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 500
# Description: Unexpected error on our end.
# Content-Type: application/json

## Links
# 200.CancelByProcessId
# Description: Cancel the image processing job if it is still in progress.
# OperationId: cancelTask
# Parameters.process_id: $response.header.X-Process-ID
# 200.DownloadInputByProcessId
# Description: Access the download link for the input image once the job is completed.
# OperationId: getDownloadInput
# Parameters.process_id: $response.header.X-Process-ID
# 200.DownloadOutputByProcessId
# Description: Access the download link for the enhanced image once the job is completed.
# OperationId: getDownloadOutput
# Parameters.process_id: $response.header.X-Process-ID
# 200.GetStatusByProcessId
# Description: Retrieve the status of your image processing job using the `process_id`.
# OperationId: getStatus
# Parameters.process_id: $response.header.X-Process-ID

Examples.Title: Download Input
Examples.Description: A presigned download link to the input image is provided in the response, as well as an expiration time (in Unix time). Input images are stored for **7 days** after the image has been uploaded.
Examples.Code:
### Download Input
# @name getDownloadInput
GET {{host}}/image/v1/download/input/{{process_id}}
X-API-Key: {{api_key}}
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 200
# Description: Presigned download URL
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: The request contains malformed data in the body, path, or query parameters.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 401
# Description: Authentication is required and has failed or has not yet been provided.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 403
# Description: The API key doesn't have permissions to perform the request.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 404
# Description: The requested resource does not exist.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 429
# Description: Too many requests hit the API too quickly. A backoff (e.g. exponential) is recommended for your requests.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 500
# Description: Unexpected error on our end.
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Download Output
Examples.Description: A presigned download link to the image is provided in the response, as well as an expiration time (in Unix time). If the image is not ready, this request will fail with a conflict error. Images are stored for **7 days** after the image has been processed.
Examples.Code:

X-API-Key: {{api_key}}

Examples.Title: Estimate
Examples.Description: Validates the request against our standard models and returns the estimated time taken to complete a given processing job and the credits that would be consumed for that job.
Examples.Code:
### Estimate
# @name postEstimate
POST {{host}}/image/v1/estimate
X-API-Key: {{api_key}}
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

{
  "category": "Enhance",
  "model": "Standard V2",
  "input_height": 0,
  "input_width": 0,
  "output_height": 0,
  "output_width": 0,
  "crop_to_fill": false,
  "output_format": "jpeg"
}

## Responses
# 200
# Description: The estimated time taken in seconds and the credits that would be consumed on process.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400
# Description: The request contains malformed data in the body, path, or query parameters.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 401
# Description: Authentication is required and has failed or has not yet been provided.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 403
# Description: The API key doesn't have permissions to perform the request.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 429
# Description: Too many requests hit the API too quickly. A backoff (e.g. exponential) is recommended for your requests.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 500
# Description: Unexpected error on our end.
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Delete All Statuses
Examples.Description: Remove all image processing job statuses. This operation can be used to delete the statuses of finished jobs, cleaning up the response of `Get All Statuses`, for example.
Examples.Code:
using var client = new TopazClient(apiKey);
var response = await client.ImageStatus.DeleteAllStatusesAsync();
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Topaz;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Delete Status
Examples.Description: Remove an image processing job status by its `process_id`. This operation can be used to delete the statuses of finished jobs, cleaning up the response of `GET Status`, for example.
Examples.Code:
### Delete Status
# @name deleteStatus
DELETE {{host}}/image/v1/status/{{process_id}}
X-API-Key: {{api_key}}
Accept: application/json

## Responses
# 204
# Description: The image processing job status was successfully deleted. No content returned.
# 400
# Description: The request contains malformed data in the body, path, or query parameters.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 401
# Description: Authentication is required and has failed or has not yet been provided.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 403
# Description: The API key doesn't have permissions to perform the request.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 404
# Description: The requested resource does not exist.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 409
# Description: The request conflicts with the current state.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 429
# Description: Too many requests hit the API too quickly. A backoff (e.g. exponential) is recommended for your requests.
# Content-Type: application/json
# 500
# Description: Unexpected error on our end.
# Content-Type: application/json

Examples.Title: Get All Statuses
Examples.Description:
Retrieve the status of all image processing jobs.

**Pagination (Highly Recommended):**
For better performance and to handle large numbers of statuses, pagination is highly recommended. Enable pagination by setting `paginated=true`. When pagination is enabled:
  - Use the `limit` parameter to control the number of results per page (default: 50, maximum: 100)
  - Use the `cursor` parameter to retrieve subsequent pages
  - The response will include pagination metadata with `next_cursor` and `has_next_page` fields

**Non-Paginated Response:**
When `paginated=false` (default) or omitted, the endpoint returns all statuses **up to 7 days old** as a simple array. This may be slow or fail for accounts with many statuses.
Examples.Setup: This example assumes `using Topaz;` is in scope and `apiKey` contains the required credential.

Examples.Title: Get Status
Examples.Description: Use this endpoint to retrieve status information for your image processing jobs.
Examples.Code:
### Get Status

GET {{host}}/image/v1/status/{{process_id}}
X-API-Key: {{api_key}}
Accept: application/json

## Responses

# Description: Status of the process
# Content-Type: application/json
# 400

# Description: Authentication is required and has failed or has not yet been provided.

Examples.Title: Lighting
Examples.Description:
The following are our lighting models used to adjust and balance your images to elevate it's quality despite the sub-optimal lighting conditions of the original photo.

Returns `process_id` and `eta` to use as an efficient way to track the progress of the image lighting adjustment job:
  - Use the `Status` endpoints to check the status of this job using the returned `process_id`.
  - Use the `Download` endpoints to download the input or processed image (once it is ready).
  - Use the `Cancel` endpoint to cancel the job while it is in progress.

The same `process_id` and `eta` are also returned in the response headers as `X-Process-ID` and `X-ETA` respectively.

> The `eta` represents when the job is expected to finish in Unix time. We recommend using this to know when you can expect your (bulk) jobs to finish processing.

Examples.Title: Restore Generative
Examples.Description:
The following are our restoration models designed to enhance photos that are older and of poorer quality.

Returns `process_id` and `eta` to use as an efficient way to track the progress of the image restoration job:
  - Use the `Status` endpoints to check the status of this job using the returned `process_id`.
  - Use the `Download` endpoints to download the input or processed image (once it is ready).
  - Use the `Cancel` endpoint to cancel the job while it is in progress.

The same `process_id` and `eta` are also returned in the response headers as `X-Process-ID` and `X-ETA` respectively.

> The `eta` represents when the job is expected to finish in Unix time. We recommend using this to know when you can expect your (bulk) jobs to finish processing.

Examples.Title: Sharpen
Examples.Description:
Returns `process_id` and `eta` to use as an efficient way to track the progress of the image sharpening job:
  - Use the `Status` endpoints to check the status of this job using the returned `process_id`.
  - Use the `Download` endpoints to download the input or processed image (once it is ready).
  - Use the `Cancel` endpoint to cancel the job while it is in progress.

The same `process_id` and `eta` are also returned in the response headers as `X-Process-ID` and `X-ETA` respectively.

> The `eta` represents when the job is expected to finish in Unix time. We recommend using this to know when you can expect your (bulk) jobs to finish processing.
Examples.Code:
### Sharpen
# @name postSharpen
POST {{host}}/image/v1/sharpen/async
X-API-Key: {{api_key}}
Content-Type: multipart/form-data
Accept: application/json
